_doc: Canonical offensive philosophies with their EXPECTED route-distribution fingerprints. Each fingerprint is a 12-route share dictionary using the harmonized taxonomy (matches data/scheme/team_route_harmonized.parquet). Used as templates to compute a 'philosophy mix' score for any team's actual route distribution. Edit by hand to refine — these are seed values informed by known practitioners, not learned from data.

_schema.label: Display name
_schema.philosophy: 1-2 sentence description of the offensive identity
_schema.expected_route_shares: Dict of route → expected share (must sum to ~1.0 across the 12-route harmonized taxonomy)
_schema.modern_practitioners: List of OCs/HCs currently or recently running this scheme
_schema.color: Hex color used in UI radar / stacked bar visualizations

WCO.label: West Coast Offense
WCO.philosophy: Short, timing-based, rhythm passing. Ball-control with high-completion%, lots of yards-after-catch. Pre-snap motion, condensed sets, run after the catch.

Air Coryell.label: Air Coryell
Air Coryell.philosophy: Vertical attack — push the ball downfield, route-tree-numbered system, RB as a receiving threat. Classic 'air it out' mentality with deep crossers and double-moves.

Erhardt-Perkins.label: Erhardt-Perkins
Erhardt-Perkins.lineage: Ron Erhardt + Ray Perkins (Giants/Patriots, 1980s) — formalized by Belichick
Erhardt-Perkins.philosophy: Concept-based — every route concept is a named entity ('Ghost', 'Dagger', 'Sail') and any player can run any spot in the concept. Adaptable, matchup-driven, often less verbal than WCO.
modern_practitioners: Matt Patricia (defensive but in this tree)

Spread/RPO.label: Spread / RPO
Spread/RPO.lineage: College Air Raid + RPO innovations (2010s) imported to NFL
Spread/RPO.philosophy: Shotgun-heavy, quick-passing concepts grafted onto run schemes. Lots of slants, screens, RPOs. Built around mobile QBs and quick-twitch decision-making.

Power Run / Vertical.label: Power Run / Vertical
Power Run / Vertical.lineage: Greg Roman (49ers/Bills/Ravens) + classic NFL run-first schemes
Power Run / Vertical.philosophy: Establish the run, build the pass game off play-action vertical shots. Heavy 12/13 personnel, designed QB runs, pin-and-pull blocking schemes. Pass attempts often longer/deeper than league average.
